transcript.clean_text: at the nine stages of the tour de france it would be today the two-time defending champion in the yellow jersey watford art bossing the points competition and wearing green german former stage winner zimon geske as the king of the mountains antares are of course also in charge of the best young writers competition to the alps they came with a 39 second difference between pogaccha and vinegar former winner thomas would be in third with yates fourth gordu fifth and the gc still relatively close the sun shining on a hot rest day in the alps but drama before the start covered positives meaning that george bennett would be unable to start the tenth stage and man down for tane pogachar and uae emirates that 10th stage a 148 kilometer run from marzin venue for a victory for yoni sagiri six years ago and a finish in merchev the first time the tour de france would take a stage in there since chris froome won a time trial in 2016. it would take well over 60 kilometers for the breakaway to go with a lightning fast start and 25 men would form the basis of the bakerway plenty of teams with one or two different riders in there and no real will to chase but nobody hugely dangerous in the general classification the best place to those would be leonard kenner the german rider he'd be at almost nine minutes down with 43 kilometers to go alberto betyal took off the gap seven minutes at this point but the gaps would then be paused climate change protesters forcing everybody to stop and they'd all be assured once told to stop that the gaps in place at this point would be reinstated once the pause button was unpressed that's able to regain his advantage with the flag wave once more 35 k's to go how would the paws have affected him behind they'd still be chasing who got offstate setting off in search of betio he wouldn't make it however and when the peloton started seven minutes later we were on the way again the leader well inside the final 30 k's at this stage 20 of those final 30 k's would be uphill and alberto betiol having 44 seconds of a gap as the road started to tilt towards the mountains fred wright would set off in search of him second time he'd be in the shake up for a stage at this particular tour after brilliant performance to lausanne just a couple of days before the rest day but there was lots of looking around lots of stop start stuff attacking here and more attacking there betty all caught but he'd have caught with him and with six kilometers to go court followed on as luis leon sanchez with the man to go out in front he himself would be caught by nick schultz and mateo jorgensen just under two kilometers remaining when that happened dylan from bala ready to come through and try and hit them with one kilometer to go the venue for the finale the runway of the altiport in merced fabala himself again caught benjamin thomas bringing a group back including magnus court who was able to go around luis leon sanchez stay in the slipstream of nick schultz and just about come around him on the finish line both riders not knowing who had won as they crossed the line the replay showed us that by around a quarter of a wheel if that magnus caught nielsen of denmark venue for the grand apar that took a stage win second in his career leonard kendall had been waiting by the side of the road to see if he'd move into the yellow jersey but by a matter of just 11 seconds telepogator held on and still leads the tour de france going into stage 11. magnus court beating nick schultz of australia luis leon sanchez of spain mateo jorgensen the american and dylan fambala the netherlander 10 seconds separating first and fifth almost nine minutes the winner and the pelican bogata then leading leonard kendall who moves up into third and bumps garrett thomas off the podium by just those 11 seconds luis leon sanchez's ride puts him into the top 10 as well but pogacha with vinegar still at 39 seconds the first serious contender the riders stay in the apps for the next couple of days and it's the first of two hugely important alpine stages coming up on stage 11. 151.7 kilometers the highest point of the race on the calder galilee and then a downhill before a finisher called granol the climb not seen in the tour since the 1980s you'll be able to see stage 11 live or on demand on gcn plus
